coverage_optimized.label: Coverage-optimized (0/30/60/90 = 30/45/20/5)
coverage_optimized.description: Balanced for general constellation coverage assumptions, with most weight at moderate beta angles.

leo_heavy.label: LEO-heavy worst-season bias
leo_heavy.description: Stress-test case that emphasizes lower beta angles, where eclipses are generally more frequent.

high_beta_favorable.label: High-beta favorable
high_beta_favorable.description: Optimistic case that shifts weight toward high-beta geometry with longer sunlight windows.

parameter_reference_cards.title: Compute mass anchor
parameter_reference_cards.detail: Notebook estimate derived from a 3,000 kg ground NVL72 system with a DGX B200 cross-check; treated as a first-pass stripped-down spacecraft packaging estimate.

parameter_reference_cards.title: Launch cost anchor
parameter_reference_cards.detail: Notebook default is a mid-range reusable-launch anchor between Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy reference points, then scales nonlinearly with transfer delta-v.

parameter_reference_cards.title: In-space transfer efficiency
parameter_reference_cards.detail: Matches the notebook kick-stage assumption for converting altitude changes into equivalent launched mass.

parameter_reference_cards.title: Solar array mass efficiency
parameter_reference_cards.detail: Anchored to the notebook's Solestial-style next-generation 50 kW-class array reference.

parameter_reference_cards.title: Radiator efficiency
parameter_reference_cards.detail: Notebook reference uses OSR-like radiator performance with 0.90 emissivity and 0.20 solar absorptivity.

parameter_reference_cards.title: Radiator weight
parameter_reference_cards.detail: First-pass notebook assumption for radiator areal density. Higher values increase both hardware mass and launch cost.

references.title: Notebook compute-mass derivation
references.detail: The notebook explains the 750 kg compute-module anchor as a stripped-down spacecraft estimate from a 3,000 kg ground NVL72 plus a DGX B200 cross-check.

references.title: Introl GB200 NVL72 Deployment Guide
references.detail: Used in the notebook for the 3,000 kg NVL72 ground mass anchor, including liquid cooling, rack frame, and AC power hardware.

references.title: NVIDIA DGX B200 User Guide
references.detail: Used in the notebook for the 142.4 kg DGX B200 server mass cross-check and other system specs.

references.title: Solestial NASA SBIR announcement
references.detail: Public anchor for next-generation 50 kW-class space solar arrays with roughly 200 W/kg specific power and about $40/W target pricing.

references.title: Military Aerospace summary of Solestial award
references.detail: Secondary public reference reinforcing the 50 kW-class, ~200 W/kg solar-array target used in the notebook.

references.title: SpaceX Falcon 9 User's Guide (May 2025)
references.detail: Payload-performance tables used as one of the notebook anchors for effective $/kg to roughly 550 km LEO.

references.title: CSIS Aerospace Security launch cost comparison
references.detail: Used in the notebook to bracket Falcon 9, Falcon Heavy, and broader LEO launch-cost reference ranges.

references.title: SpaceX Starship overview
references.detail: Used only for future-sensitivity presets in the web tool, not as a current procurement price quote.

references.title: Notebook in-space transfer assumption
references.detail: The notebook sets 320 s Isp and 12% propulsion dry-mass fraction as a first-order kick-stage assumption for altitude-raising sensitivity analysis.

references.title: Notebook radiator optical-property assumption
references.detail: Radiator emissivity 0.90 and low solar absorptivity are explicit notebook assumptions intended to represent an OSR-like high-performance radiator surface.

references.title: Notebook radiator areal-density assumption
references.detail: Radiator areal density of 2.0 kg/m² is a first-pass modeling assumption in the notebook and should be treated as editable sensitivity input, not a fixed industry standard.
